title: Slab-built oval planter
subtitle: A four-wall slab planter with a drainage insert, sealed for outdoor shelf use
excerpt: A medium oval planter built from five clay slabs (four walls and a base) with an inner drainage insert to keep the outer clay body dry. Sealed air-dry clay survives a sheltered outdoor spot; an inner terracotta or plastic pot handles water. Intermediate difficulty because of accurate corner joining.
sourceNotes: Slab-building technique derived from first principles.

glossaryTerms.definition: A flat sheet of clay rolled to an even thickness.
The drying stage where clay holds its shape but is still damp enough to join.
Scratching joining surfaces and brushing them with slurry before pressing together.

content.text: A slab-built planter uses the same construction logic as the lidded box but scaled up and shaped for a decorative outer pot. The key principle: air-dry clay is not waterproof, so the outer clay planter holds a smaller terracotta or plastic inner pot that handles all the watering. The clay body stays dry.

items.name: Inner nursery pot to use as reference

content.text: to 6 mm thickness. Cut the following pieces to fit a 13 cm by 10 cm inner nursery pot (adjust to match your inner pot):
Two long walls: 15 cm by 12 cm
Two short walls: 12 cm by 12 cm (each adds 6 mm for wall thickness)
1. Join the walls to the base
on every join. Stand the long walls on the base first. Then fit the short walls into the open ends. Reinforce all interior corners with thin coils pressed flat with a rib.
While the walls are still soft, press stamps, a leaf, or coarse linen into the exterior faces for texture.

Roll four short cylinders of clay, each 8 mm diameter and 15 mm tall. Score and slurry the base of the planter at the corners and press a cylinder foot at each corner. These raise the planter off the shelf for drainage and airflow.
Let the assembled planter firm to

under light plastic cover, then dry open for two to three days. Once bone-dry, apply three coats of exterior-grade acrylic varnish or PVA, covering all surfaces including the base. Three coats provides enough water resistance for a sheltered outdoor position.
Keep the outer planter dry: water the plant through the inner pot only. Bring indoors during heavy rain or frost. Air-dry clay, even sealed, is not suitable for full outdoor exposure.

items.cause: Corners were not reinforced with coils or score-and-slip was skipped
items.fix: Press paper clay slurry into the crack; allow to dry slowly

items.cause: Base dried faster than the walls
items.fix: Weight the base with a flat ceramic tile while drying; or dry upside-down on the base so the base dries last
